context: QA failure remediation - 5 gaps fixed in single cycle

what_worked_well: Red-phase checksum system detected test file modification (TEST_INTEGRITY_MISMATCH on test_ac5_config.sh)
TDD phase state tracking enforced complete 10-phase workflow execution despite QA failure
Remediation workflow was targeted and efficient - fixed 5 specific gaps without scope expansion
All 54 tests passing after single remediation cycle (50 original + 4 new)
areas_for_improvement: HARDCODED_PATH anti-pattern recurs in test files despite context file guidance - test-automator not enforcing relative-path constraint during Phase 02
FUNCTION_ORDERING (bash-specific): log() called in ERR trap before definition - framework lacks shellcheck integration in Phase 04
AC#6 specified 'step IDs' but STORY-525 (step registry) not yet built - AC written beyond implementation capability
Non-functional AC requirements (AC#5 'Existing hooks unchanged') not decomposed into separate test assertions during Phase 02

recommendations.title: Add shellcheck validation to Phase 04 Refactor for bash scripts
Create relative-path validation rule for test file generation in Phase 02
Validate prerequisite story completion status during story creation
Decompose non-functional AC requirements into separate test cases during Phase 02

patterns_observed: Red-phase checksum system effective at detecting test modifications within QA Phase 1.5
QA deep validation catches multi-layer violations: code quality, portability, specification gaps
Remediation-triggered test additions are legitimate but violate RED-phase integrity model
anti_patterns_detected: HARDCODED_PATH recurrence in test files despite anti-patterns.md guidance
AC specification beyond implementation capability (AC#6 before STORY-525)
Non-functional requirements bundled with functional tests lacking dedicated assertions
ai_analysis.constraint_analysis: Context files provided clear guidance but subagent enforcement is inconsistent. All issues caught by QA but could have been prevented earlier in Phase 02/04.
